mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals strong aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as firms benefit from elevated economic activity.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are negative when both INDPRO and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising Fed funds rates above 4.5% compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS surpasses 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate profits face downward pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO declines by over 1.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and falling inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates favorable demand-supply dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 2-year window, or 20-day returns are negative when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL falls.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment rate below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that push wage growth and inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 3% in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with stable inflation indicates robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings benefit from elevated demand.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are negative when GDPC1 rises and CPIAUCSL remains stable.
